payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, well for a round of drinks and the milks involved as well, that'll put you we'll say at about... two silver.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Y-you tell me where you're from and why it brought you in to my table, then m-maybe I'll tell you how I lost this, yeah?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Well I'm from up the street kind of. I'm the daughter of the Ruby of the Sea. Do you work for any government officials?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm the daughter of the Ruby of the Sea. And you know these are my friends and we've gotten into a lot of hijinks and um... what all did you want to know?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Drink gets brought down to the table. "Don't worry, she's going to take care of it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Do you have money to take care of it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You've got it? Because I was going to go get some milk too. Two milks also. One hot please. Two milks, one hot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a silver for that so it's an easy trade off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We order anything other than milk please.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He turns and you hear the creaking of leather as the long extended neck turns, the folds of skin pushing against themselves. "Oh this? Got this here on sea maybe about... six or seven years ago."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Drink gets brought down to the table. "Don't worry, she's going to take care of it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Do you have money to take care of it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You've got it? Because I was going to go get some milk too. Two milks also. One hot please. Two milks, one hot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a silver for that so it's an easy trade off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We order anything other than milk please.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, well for a round of drinks and the milks involved as well, that'll put you we'll say at about... two silver.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's also possible that we met my dad earlier and we didn't know it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm going to be perfectly honest  that was a lot m-more than I was asking. But I appreciate it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I appreciate you too. How did you get those pipes on your back? (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "It's been a number of m-m-months since I found me a good ship and a purpose out there on the ocean sea, but I've been known to be a rather decent second mate and navigator."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, fake bagpipe sound is best bagpipe sound. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What is happening? I don't know anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He's so good! That was good!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Only a few notes in because while he's mischievously enjoying the moment of shattering the calm and chill in the chamber he's not going to get himself thrown out, so he stops and goes "That there's how I keep the other shipmates... entertained."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a very good skill, you're quite talented. Really.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What's your typical rank on board ship if you don't mind me asking?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "(laughs) M-M-Marius ain't really what you'd call sea-trekking folk. More of a slimy, shadow word peddler. However, last time I saw him not but maybe a week ago, I recall he was complaining. Waiting on something to be delivered. Never found its way around. Was getting real-- that boy owes a lot of money."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well it's complicated, I'm not even going to pretend that I entirely understand it myself. We're--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's a present. It was a present for him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's a shifting to Orly at which point the last of the air in the small pouch gets pushed in and it (wailing bagpipes) slowly comes to rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Again I hate to ask personal questions but--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Bastard. Have you ever sailed with Marius by any chance? I mean, do you have an impression of him as a sailor or--?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, we'll say one silver for just her, for one round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys approach, with Fjord towards the front, she watches you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Couldn't help but notice we seemed to have caught your eye.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I'm going to be honest. It's hard not to draw my attention with that very subtle display you've all managed to provide."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I apologize. Clearly my friends and I are new in town, and we are very inquisitive. What is that you're drinking?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) Am I slipping? I can't help it! I can't not do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, would you care for more?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll wave to the barkeep to bring another round over.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For just her or for both of you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That'll put you back, we'll say--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Oh no, just passing through. Taking a break. I've only been here for about a week, and then shoving off back to this trade route towards Marquet, probably."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, we'll say one silver for just her, for one round.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: If you wouldn't mind, we were moving through the town, and there is this tower right in the middle of it. The Tide Peak, I believe it's called in my notes. Can you tell me, do you know anything about that? Such a strange structure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "To be completely honest, I don't spend a lot of time in the Quay. I tend to focus more on this side. The business is better." I don't know why I'm focused on that other eye. I'm stuck in that. I just noticed that. No, it's good. She was leaning this way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know, and it's a problem already. She goes, "I've seen it. I've certainly gazed and seen that weird tower. I think that's what you're referring to."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you live in these parts, or are you just passing through?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I maybe got room for one more in the crew if you want to come along. Prove yourself a few days at sea."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know, and it's a problem already. She goes, "I've seen it. I've certainly gazed and seen that weird tower. I think that's what you're referring to."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you live in these parts, or are you just passing through?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh no, just passing through. Taking a break. I've only been here for about a week, and then shoving off back to this trade route towards Marquet, probably."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Indeed. Not for some time, I'm a bit out of practice, but yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What's your specialty? I might be looking for crew."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm quite a strong back. Good with rigging, topsail. You name it, I've done it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I didn't quite make it up past making the crew. I'm afraid higher duty didn't quite suit me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Could be. Do you have work?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "She's been through a lot, but she's stuck with me for many years."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What's your specialty? I might be looking for crew."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm quite a strong back. Good with rigging, topsail. You name it, I've done it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I didn't quite make it up past making the crew. I'm afraid higher duty didn't quite suit me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Could be. Do you have work?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I maybe got room for one more in the crew if you want to come along. Prove yourself a few days at sea."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I might stop by. Where is your ship?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She glances off to the side and points on the far left side of the harbor. "I can't really show you from here. Follow me." She steps up and walks past you towards the front and points to one ship. "Past there, the one with the grayish sails furled up. That's the Drensala Vis. That's my ship."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I think when we round up with a full crew and supplies gathered up. Probably want to take some time to relax here in town for another week or so and then we'll head off. It's probably going to be a good six, seven months' journey before we come back. But if you and your accountant friend are looking to come, we got room for two more."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is my accountant, Caleb Widogast. Very intelligent, good with books and numbers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've got to say, I'm pretty intrigued by a strong-backed, small-ranked sailor looking for work that walks around with his own accountant."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well. not specifically to Fjord, but to our group. We are an odd bunch, a collection of people, so you know, we tend to get our noses in things.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You know how valuable it is to be a jack of all trades. I've tried some things in the meantime while I've been landlocked.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: When we need to be at sea, we have Fjord. When we need to crunch numbers, we have me, and we have others as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I'm just Captain Adella. Pleasure to meet you."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The pleasure is all ours. When are you planning to cast off?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Yeah, half-elf fella? Yeah, he was in here a few months back when I was last docked in Nicodranas. Working with that lowlife, Cadmus. Haven't seen him since, though.".

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's about as much as I've heard. I tend not to poke around places that act weird, strange magics and such. Lot of folks that get too curious end up not coming back."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) --that badass, hot captain. And now she's older! She's got the salt and pepper hair! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was waiting for a fluorescent light to turn on. No, it was good, you were engaged.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wasn't she the one that I asked if she was a pirate, right? She was like, "The fuck?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we sailed with her. Vox Machina sailed with her.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just curious, you don't happen to have any members of your crew by the name of Sabian, do you?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, about five minutes of back and forth you get to the point where you're both laughing and feel comfy. Eventually he goes, "Don't even worry about the money, just, I'll keep an eye on out here and don't take it too far out in the bay. When you're done, bring it back."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Old timer! I'm sorry to bother you, I don't see a mariner's office around here. Is that your rowboat?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh yeah, this here is my boat. What's your name, sir?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Little bit, little bit, my friends here were hoping to explore the coast a bit. And if I could put some coin in your pocket, I was wondering if you would mind parting with your vessel here for a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We'll say, because you have a sailor's background at this point, you guys converse back and forth a bit. You swap some stories with him about how he used to be a dockworker, and some of the ships he was on back in the day.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tales of the worst greenhorns you've ever worked with.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (sheepishly) "I mean, there's been-- You know. About a year ago they had this one ship that came into the-- There were these thieves-- And I happened to be on job at the moment and so-- yeah!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wow! I hear they're haunted. Are they haunted?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, not haunted, per se. But there are--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, you gave me a little smirk. They're totally haunted! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes, "Not haunted, but there are elements to it--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes, "There also-- There are things that help maintain the Sluice ducts, and occasionally help protect elements of the bay if ever it's under attack."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I bet you're first in line to protect the city against an attack, aren't you?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Of course. What are you doing right now? I only have about 40 minutes until I get to switch my shift, and then I'm off for the rest of the afternoon."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From this distance it's too hard to see beneath the water. It gets a little too murky. But he points in a general direction and you get a loose idea of where it may be.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's like, "So what are you up to while you're in the city?" (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wow! Yeah, you know, I'm just so busy. You know, just trying to fill my schedule with things. I should probably go now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (forced giggling) I'm staying at the Lavish Chateau!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, wow! That is a very expensive place."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I know. You probably couldn't afford it, so--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can see the visible (pained grunt) to his chest. He's like, "Well, perhaps I'll-- If you want other things to see around the city when I'm not on my post, I could maybe come by and give you a little tour?"

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It works out, just so you guys know, your constitution modifier is how many minutes you can hold your breath underwater.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you guys go ahead and come back onto it again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did not think that was going to work.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I didn't either, but it's open.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, here's my other question, though can we hold our breath that long?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did it seem like it was hard to hold our breath while we were doing all of that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, you guys all have pretty high constitutions, so not really.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Should we try the other one?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's try the other one!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That ends Fjord's. It's now their turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'll throw two Eldritch Blasts to this creature behind Caduceus.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The one that appeared behind the rest? Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just hits. I'll roll the second one, well fuck. That's 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12 misses. So, roll damage on the first strike.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Gotcha. Fuck you. Five points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Five points of damage. The Eldritch Blast hits it, and you watch as it creates this circle in the center of its elemental form as it impacts and actually strikes the stone behind with whatever residual energy from the original impact. The hole forms up. It took damage. It didn't seem to be as resistant as it had to previous blows. Magic seems to cut through whatever defense it has.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As it emerges, you feel the water beneath you begin to whip into a torrent, and you are swept off of your feet and knocked prone and are considered currently restrained as it moves into your space. It's now currently swirling around you, and you're having a hard time moving. It's now forcing water into your mouth. You can't breathe in the moment. You are whelmed by the elemental.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12 misses. So, roll damage on the first strike.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Gotcha. Fuck you. Five points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Five points of damage. The Eldritch Blast hits it, and you watch as it creates this circle in the center of its elemental form as it impacts and actually strikes the stone behind with whatever residual energy from the original impact. The hole forms up. It took damage. It didn't seem to be as resistant as it had to previous blows. Magic seems to cut through whatever defense it has.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That ends Fjord's. It's now their turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This one, who currently is engaged directly with you, is going to wisp around into the water and emerge on the other side of Beau and is going to go ahead and attempt to Whirlpool Strike you. I need you to make a strength saving throw, please.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll allow it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten damage. Very good. So with that, as you're standing there, you go ahead and swing one fist down and it strikes right at the center of it, and you watch as it separates for a second. As it reforms, you go for another strike, and this time it manages to duck just out of the way, seeing the precursor of that strike, and it dodges entirely. That finishes your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. That brings us to Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits. Go ahead and roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That sucks. This plus four is five!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And then I'm going to use Guiding Bolt at the other one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And I'm assuming-- tell me if I'm wrong-- because it's a flash of light that streaks towards the creature, that would give enough light for Caduceus to send something out?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All righty. It's now their turn. So this one here, now low on health and a bit frenzied, is going to go ahead and take two strikes at the enlarged Beauregard that has been its issue at this time. That's going to be a 21 to hit and a 22 to hit. 15 and 16 naturally.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I approach the water fuck that's on Mr. Clay? And I will use Booming Blade against it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So at 5th level it deals 2d8 thunder damage to the target.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Four. So eight, plus whatever the modifier is. Or it's just 2d8, right? Booming Blade is still an attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah that's it, that's just 2d8. So eight points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Eight points of damage. (impact noise) It is currently encircled by the booming energy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (effeminate voice) What's that, Travis, what's that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. That ends your turn, Fjord?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It breaks fairly easy. The wood is very soaked and warped. And as your foot breaks through it, it's now up to the knee inside of the barrel. Doesn't shatter, you're now stuck with your leg partially in a barrel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What size are the stones? Just curious.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They're a pound each. What are you talking about? I'm talking about--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I just tap the stone. Ten hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Where does that next tunnel go?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The one that had to be there? Go ahead and make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a lot of water ahead. And some walls.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's just a lot of water and I think just more stone walls.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is there an intense discoloration, maybe, of the mold underneath the grate? Is it a different kind of--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, on the smaller one, you mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to kick through the barrel while he's doing that. Did I find a gold?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You all quietly get low into the water and off to the sides of the entrance to best stay out of range of the light source. You guys feel pretty confident that you are, for the most part, out of range of anyone who isn't fairly perceptive. You can see the faint dull glow from the center far end of the chamber. Now that you're closer and can see, there are two similar pillars in the center of the room. The last room you were in, they were just load-bearing center bits. There are some small elements of almost walkways or little bits of stone that emerge from the far wall, and the corners that either rubble has built up or it's been intentionally built as some sort of platform there. That's off the water.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Moving up to the very edge of it, you can see the interior of this chamber and the display that Caleb had described. Caduceus, as you guys are stepping up, you hear this faint echoing sound emerging from the upper left hallway. You see a very faint bit of light coming, and you hear talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I cast Pass Without a Trace. Do I have that? I do. Yeah, I cast Pass Without a Trace on all of us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're going to stealth, right? Because I just cast it on all of us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, and I give Caduceus the Blessing of the Trickster.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, everyone make stealth checks, please.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As the water bows up, you see it burst like a bubble, and there, emerging from that space below where it was, you see something that looks like an impressively large aquatic humanoid, their chest and torso rather rotund, their skin a blue-green color, long thin arms that end in somewhat webbed, clawed fingers. Their face somewhat fish-like, with a wide mouth and small fins that crest the head. The lower half of its body seems obscured by rushing water at first, but then as it comes to its full stance at about seven, eight foot tall, you notice that the lower half of its body is just water. It's a constant swirling whirlwind, similar to the creatures you were fighting in the previous chamber. That dull glow seems to emanate from most of its torso in this darkened chamber, and it gives this faint light source by its very presence. It has a small vest on, and sets of jewelry, including this thick metal band that hangs like a presentory necklace around its throat. It turns and looks up and says, "At your service." It seems to say with an undertone of exasperation and eyeroll. Algar screams out to it again. "The Archway craftsman are complaining of cold furnaces and slow bellows. Please ensure enough water is being spun through the engines into the district." He snaps his fingers really tightly on his left hand. With a low grunt of brimming hatred, the creature raises its arms and a large stationary wave of water rises from the depths beside it. The liquid seems to form once more and there's a slight sparkle of energy around its hands, and this second bowing forms into a large curved wave that seems to be perpetually crashing and then filling itself, similar to the creatures you just fought, but a much larger scale. It whips into its full form and where the head would be bends and shifts-- almost elements out of the movie The Abyss-- as it turns toward this figure being referred to as Zsundie. It spins around and looks up towards the supervisor and his troupe. "Thy will is my bond." The two of them begin to turn and shift towards the left hand grate in the chamber.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, because I was like ten seconds away from casting Eyes of the Grave.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I want to listen, but I shouldn't listen.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Tickets are still on sale, orchestra seats, very few left.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: East coast, come bring it! Boston, Massachusetts.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Who knows what will happen at the live show?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Algar shouts it off to Zsundie down below. Let's roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What is the distance from where we are to the terrace balcony space that they're on?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'd say roughly 80 or so feet. At this point, Zsundie comes to a stop and looks over his shoulder in your direction and goes, "Oh. Intruders." Algar shouts off, "What? How?" and holds up his lantern and focuses the beam over across the way there. You guys, even though you're up against the wall, there's nothing to hide behind. The light pushes past a few of you and then stops. He shouts off, "Dispatch them quickly!"

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, roll for initiative, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Matt's gone, I guess. Oh, he's back!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me for a moment of preparation here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's real water. And colored lights!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is the difficulty of doing this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you think we were going to get to this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I didn't know if you were. This is the difficulty of doing this in here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know, that's all right. We will fix this, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You sounded like Bob Ross as he was saying that. It's all happening nicely.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'd like you guys to place yourselves, if you can, where you would be on this side of the chamber as you enter. There is Zsundie.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And there is the large elemental where you had seen him. The worker has run past and vanished into the chamber before, and there on the top, looking over the terrace, the dwarven bodyguards right there. And that's Algar right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he's the tall one right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Matt, I'm putting Frumpkin in this corner.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're not in the room, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, we haven't come in yet. We were all hiding in this little thing. I'm putting you in the back, Nott.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So you can hop up. Oh, that's true. That was our lineup.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was stealthing so hard, I'm playing with the McElroys right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For that right there? To Algar, from you? Somewhere in the neighborhood of 60 to 70 feet. You're not entirely sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, got to roll for initiative.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll allow you to make it as far as here without using any movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The little things, really. All right. So--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Turn that down just a little bit, there. So, guys--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It was worth it for me. It was worth it. Thank you for being patient on that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I want to do some parkour, awesome dope monk shit, and be like (repeating) bink. And see if I can get to here, 40 feet?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an acrobatics check. If you want to try. With disadvantage, because they're slick, moist walls.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Real good on both ones! I'll take that 16 from Gil. I take it all back, Gil.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That ends Beau's turn. It is now the water elemental's turn. Vanishes beneath the water.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck it, this is what I worked for. I'm going to go for it and try and do two shurikens right at his face. Three-quarters cover. First one is a 14 plus seven, so that's a big old 21! And then nine plus seven is a big old 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. With three-quarters cover, that's going to be a plus five to AC, so his AC is technically-- what were the two rolls?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah Algar, don't mess with the Ruby.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The first one-- mm-hmm, nine damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Actually, yeah-- they don't have a moment to react. Cool, so that ends your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You throw both. One ricochets off, the other one (grunt), Algar looks, "What the hell? Get them!"

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, top of the round. Nott, you're up. Actually, no, the dwarves have to go still. The bodyguards rush over, getting into the space that that's there, pushing past to the-- heads this way. This one is going to move back and you don't know what this one does because he's moved from your visual range, but this one, as it pulls back, it has a large heavy crossbow, and it's going to go ahead and take a shot at you, Jester, from back here. Actually, let's see. No, he's going to shoot at Caduceus because Caduceus is already wounded and wants to try and take down those who are already hit. That's going to be, against Caduceus-- it's going to be a 21 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Still the lollipop, but razor blade edges.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try to hit his arm and chop off his arm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and roll some damage on that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did that work? Did I chop off his arm because that's what I was trying to do!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't actually see him, so you're hoping it hits across the wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm hoping it hits him! Ten damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Technically, I should have given you disadvantage because you can't see the target, but it's fine, I'll let it go through. There you go. You hear (groan) across the wall. You have no idea to what effect it was, but you hit something. Probably Algar.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you a fan of Matisse?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 22? I'll allow you to just get the final distance on the edge and have to correct yourself and then catch it on the ledge. But that's the end of your movement there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is this disadvantage again or no? I'm not rolling Gil.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is still disadvantage because it's slick.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't you have a feat now? Fanatical fanatic?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you land, and slip right into the ground, the water splashes, you're underwater for a second. You use the rest of your Step of the Wind extra dash movement to get up from prone. That's your bonus action.You still have your action and movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I move the rest of the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can't jump now? I can't use that double jump distance? Is that done, Step of the Wind?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll say if you wanted to try and vertical leap from here, go ahead and make an athletics check to see if, given the circumstances of the turn, you can make it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, you have like three minutes before you drown, sitting there. So you're okay for now. We'll say, for just the purposes of like, you're not sucking air in necessarily.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So we just got to say every game, we just have to make sure that we spit it out. Okay, so I fail, I got a seven.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so that's 25 points of bludgeoning damage to you, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Guess who's going to be healing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm not out, because I have Molly's necklace.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You have the necklace from Molly!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're out, you're just not doing death saves.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're unconscious, but you're not making death saves, you're stabilized.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's one foot of water, you probably fell on your back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's got to keep stabilizing you, I think.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, you can still drown with it on, but what's your constitution modifier?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, it's only when you're using hit dice during a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh my God this is happening again!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I also have a question about the Periapt. It says that whenever you roll-- oh, it's rolling a hit die to regain hit points, but it doesn't count when anyone heals me?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Usually you say that at the top of the turn but it's been a while so you can do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So the first attack was what?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: The first attack was 16, but it was cocked.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 19 hits. So go ahead and roll damage for that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That was such a gentle roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Don't even-- I'm not rolling another character.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 18 points of damage on the first strike. You carve through, you watch as a big piece of it splashes away. It's looking hurt. It's not heavy, but you're starting to see some wear and tear on the creature. You make your second attack?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Does he look like he's getting loose at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it looks like he's still held in there.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, fuck. And I don't get to roll again, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you don't have a means for getting advantage. Remember, you have your reckless strikes.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes, let me do reckless.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Is that what you wanted to do?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That is a six? We're going to go ahead and... say that you are in the beginning stages of drowning. So you can survive a number of rounds equal to your constitution modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's right, the beetles would have probably clambered back into the staff when you fell unconscious.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's okay, I was just hoping for even like advantage or some such.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to back up a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, will it get an attack of opportunity?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'll just stay where I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you just continue engaged and holding it in that moment. Okay, that ends Yasha's turn. Algar is going to make a death saving throw. That's a failure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We don't want him to die.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't think he was clear on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So what do I do? Concentration check, death save? I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, make a constitution saving throw.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: We'll see where it goes. We'll see where it goes in two rounds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, yeah, at the-- if you run out of breath or are choking you survive the number of rounds equal to your constitution modifier. At the start of your next turn, you drop to zero hit points and are dying.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so it's not terrible. It's bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It gets a little weird. It gets a little weird here. Because you're stabilized. You're stabilized by the necklace.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So can he drown and then he stabilizes and then he drowns and stabilizes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Kind of. It gets a little weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Kind of. So yeah, you're stabilized.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, there are dragons in this world.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, Cure Wounds is still an action, so you can't do both in one round, unfortunately.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Kind of. So yeah, you're stabilized.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, there are dragons in this world.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We'll see where it goes. We'll see where it goes in two rounds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. That ends Caleb's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have faith in you, Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to step forward. I'm going to stay away from the water thing. I'm going to use my duplicate to go forward and touch Caduceus.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And she's going to cast Cure Wounds at third level.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So that is (counting) 19 points of health.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And then with me, I'm going to Sacred Flame. For myself.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A success and a fail-- no, both successes actually. Both rolled really high that round. All right, so two damage to each, actually. All right, and they can't see anything. They both haven't moved since the last thing happened, so one reaches down and you hear some rummaging. He gets dragged--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, does that end your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That brings us to the compatriots here. At the top of their turn they take how much damage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 2d6? Go ahead and roll 2d6. For both of the dwarven bodyguards.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Could I have used my Hellish Rebuke? If he didn't actually hit me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can use your reaction to, you can, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! I want to use my Hellish Rebuke.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, no, if it didn't hit you I don't think. Does it say an attack or a hit? I believe it has to be a hit, doesn't it? When you're damaged?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: They do take-- if they do their dexterity saving throw they take half. Again. It says that if you--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The water elemental's turn. It's now Zsundie's turn. Zsundie is going to fly into the air right up here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, because it's a racial ability?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Half damage! Okay so that's still 3d10 of cold damage

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a lot! You could freeze it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, reduces its speed by 20 feet until the end of its next turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That was, I wouldn't say hellish.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As it reaches and gets a little frozen and its movement is a little sluggish. It's looking really hurt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 13 points of bludgeoning damage. And because technically Caduceus is still--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the top of the turn. No, because you got healed by Jester so you also take 13 points of bludgeoning damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's all I needed was just to survive the round.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. First damage to Zsundie. (explosion) As the flames subside and pull back, the evaporated water in the vicinity turns into this cloud of mist that (hisses) around. Steam fills the upper chamber for a minute. And as the steam pulls away from Zsundie, barely scathed by the Fireball, (growls) just glaring right at you, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. From the ceiling down to him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Back corner of the room here, you mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah, you can do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I turn into the fetal position to cower and cover.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I think I'm at 16. Let me just double-check. Okay, yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and roll damage. He takes half damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (counting) Okay, so ten points of fire damage.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It is now--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. He's now pinned against the wall, your knee against the jaw and the chin; he's choking against the pressure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I'm going to just full-on grapple him down and hold him for my action.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh oh! Oh, can I pull his bracelet off?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You had to remember. Make an athletics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But it was in my head! But it was in my head!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an athletics check to grapple.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Dammit! It was-- I thought about it five minutes ago and I forgot-- that's good, though. That's good. 19!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he is fully grappled by you and shoved against the wall. That ends your go.
